questions.question: What is the difference between EC2 and Lambda?
questions.answer: EC2: Virtual servers you manage, always running, pay per hour. Lambda: Serverless functions, run on-demand, pay per invocation, auto-scales to zero. Lambda ideal for event-driven, short tasks. EC2 for persistent workloads.
questions.explanation: Lambda has 15min max execution, 10GB memory limit. EC2 gives full OS control. For web servers, EC2 or containers are typical. Lambda excels for async event processing, APIs with variable traffic.
